pullRequest.title: Fixing the long standing "transparency bug"
pullRequest.body:
From my anecdotal testing, this should close #90.

---

**The issue seems to arise when Windows tries to rescale the UI based on the monitor's DPI scaling.**

I run a fairly non-standard monitor setup (two 1360x768 at 100% scaling and one 1920x1080 at 125% scaling) and dragging the window from my smaller monitors to the larger one causes the window to go transparent while it readjusts the UI scaling. 

I was running a slightly older build when I discovered this bug (the UI sometimes failing to repaint, leaving it permanently transparent) and an update somewhere down the line seems to have "fixed" it by locking the window in place while it rescales. While this does work, it makes the UI feel a bit unresponsive (especially with the whole thing going transparent for a few seconds).

**The DPI scaling being the cause would also explain why both people with multiple monitors _and_ people remoting into systems get this issue.**

---

**My solution pretty much just tells Windows, "hey, don't rescale this" and it seems to work fine.**

It uses ctypes, which is already built into python (meaning it doesn't require an extra library to be added).
It includes a pass as well, in case it ends up not working for some strange reason (falling back to the already "working" version).

---

The only issue I could see would be odd UI scaling based on different resolutions/scaling because of this change (which shouldn't affect 95% of users). I haven't notice any unusable UI elements with my set up though.

**Edit - Setting it to `2` defaults back to the original fix, so it's not really a solution.
I'll still leave it here to show that it was attempted and does not work.**

It could probably be adjusted to enable per-monitor DPI awareness, but I haven't personally tested it.

```
import ctypes
try:
    ctypes.windll.shcore.SetProcessDpiAwareness(2)  # 1=system DPI aware, 2=per-monitor DPI aware
except Exception:
    pass
```
[And Microsoft strongly prefers declaring DPI awareness in the application manifest](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/hidpi/setting-the-default-dpi-awareness-for-a-process), but this shouldn't be an issue if there's not a plan to build an exe.

---

As for Linux/MacOS compatibility, this fix could technically be adjusted to this:

```
import sys
import ctypes

if sys.platform == "win32":
    try:
        ctypes.windll.shcore.SetProcessDpiAwareness(1)  # System DPI aware
    except Exception:
        pass
```

But it feels a bit excessive (since the try/except block will just silently fail regardless).
The option is there though.

---

Anyways, give it a shot and see if it works.

Even though this bug seems to be mostly a non-issue nowadays, I still find this fix to make the UI feel more "snappy" when moving between monitors.

pullRequest.title: Quick qol fixes
pullRequest.body: Quick win QoL changes to close outstanding feats or bugs. 1. Conda support (raised in discord) 2. Label displays whether model and TE's are/arent being trained. 3. Label displays ETA after certain number of steps + samples. 4. Specify UTF-8 encoding on launch scripts for windows. 5. Fixes concept window to accept empty files. 6. Validation in the learning rate widgets. 7. Tweaks export, tensorboard and train buttons and adds wholistic debug button that creates a zip containing the config.json, debug_report.log and the config_diff.txt. 8. Start Training button is colored based on state for better UX ### Relevant to point 7 and 8 ### Snippet that shows config_diff.txt ``` training_method: FINE_TUNE ⇒ LORA model_type: STABLE_DIFFUSION_15 ⇒ STABLE_DIFFUSION_XL_10_BASE debug_mode: False ⇒ True base_model_name: stable-diffusion-v1-5/stable-diffusion-v1-5 ⇒ E:/stable-diffusion-webui-forge/models/Stable-diffusion/sd_xl_base_1.0_0.9vae.safetensors weight_dtype: FLOAT_32 ⇒ FLOAT_16 ``` Relevant issues: closes #113, fixes #903, fixes #884, closes #836, closes #685

pullRequest.title: Graceful fail for sampling if config becomes corrupted or missing after training
pullRequest.body: Fixes #327 Decided to split this fix out of Dataset Tools rework given its immense size.

pullRequest.title: Fix for off-by-one error with v_prediction
pullRequest.body:
It should fix all samplers for v_prediction/zero-terminal SNR models with standard models. Most likely that's just step that most schedulers do on it's own so we did end up with duplicated step.

Models using squaredcos_cap_v2 scheduler still won't sample with kerras scheduler as sigmas are rounded that's why depending on model timesteps off-by one error can still appear with those models. Fixing it would require rewritting some sampling code, but that's chunk of work for models that do not exist.

pullRequest.title: Multi-GPU support
pullRequest.body:
**Multi-GPU support for OneTrainer**

This is a draft, but it is intended to be feature-complete and work with all models, optimizations and other parameters OT has to offer.

Some basic tests have been done to ensure that multi-GPU training learns equally well as single GPU training, by comparing

- validation loss of training on 4 GPUs
- with training on 1 GPU, but with 4x the batch size
- and with training on 1 GPU, but 4x the gradient accumulation

More testing is necessary though.

Performance impact for LoRA training is negligable, meaning that 4 GPUs train as fast as 1 GPU but with 4x the batch size:
![grafik](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6a64da80-1c5f-4a94-8cc5-f3c89caa030e)

For full fine-tuning a model as large as Flux there is some performance impact. A minimum local batch size of 4 to 8 is required for it to make sense:
![grafik](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1276ea89-fa2b-4793-af3e-929f2a58b548)

Or better hardware: This test has been done on A5000s, which are older PCIe cards similar to 3090s, connected through PIX/PXB. If anyone has access to high-end hardware with NVLink connections it would be interesting to see the remaining performance impact on full finetuning.

Multi-GPU training *should* also work on Windows (even though less optimized, because NVIDIA nccl is not available for windows), but I have no way to test that. If anyone has a windows machine with 2 GPUs at home please confirm.

This PR includes https://github.com/Nerogar/OneTrainer/pull/804

pullRequest.title: Chroma
pullRequest.body:
Use and test:
`git clone https://github.com/dxqb/OneTrainer -b chroma`
`./update.sh`
Select the #chroma preset

select a LoRA layer filter, not full, otherwise `distilled_guidance_layer` is trained which is not recommended.

code comments:
 - Chroma is different enough from Flux that I made it a separate model, instead of changing the Flux model code
 - therefore there is a lot of code duplication - but this duplication also exists between other models, so I've decided against touching that in this PR. More code sharing between models can be done as a separate PR

open points:
- [x]  support Chroma timestep distribution? https://huggingface.co/lodestones/Chroma?not-for-all-audiences=true#tldr-a-custom-timestep-distribution-prevents-loss-spikes-during-training. LOGIT_NORMAL seems close enough though for small finetunes - *done*
- [x] exclude the `distilled_guidance_layer` from training. For LoRA training the layer filter can be used, but not for full finetuning. Hardcode this exception, or add a layer filter for full finetuning? https://github.com/Nerogar/OneTrainer/pull/778 - *done, hardcoded for now*
- [x] Bug: only works if Debug is switched off - *fixed*
- [x] compare sampling parameters between Comfy/Swarm and OneTrainer - appears to be different on average - *they are indeed different, but model creator has confirmed that our parameters are correct*
- [ ] Chroma seems to scale differently based on sequence length than Flux. Is this expected? Compare with other trainers?
```
Chroma bs2 512 2s/it
Chroma bs2 1024 10.3 s/it
Flux bs2 512 2.1s/it
Flux bs2 1024 8.7 s/it
```


- [x] "Force Attention Mask" is not useful with Chroma; remove
- [x] implement https://github.com/Nerogar/OneTrainer/pull/770 for Chroma
- [x] there are reports that embedding training fails on Chroma - could be an issue with the code, or inherent to the model. Check embedding vectors during training - *everything seems ok*
- [x] Loading Chroma from single file safetensors fails, probably a diffusers bug

pullRequest.title: Change offloading alignment
pullRequest.body:
CUDA seems to expected tensors to be aligned to 8 bytes, not only to 4 bytes. When offloading transfers tensors from CPU back to GPU, it currently only aligns to 4 bytes.

This likely fixes #827

pullRequest.title: fix for #847
pullRequest.body:
- don't expect audio and video formats when you sample an image
 - load VAE always in float32 to avoid type mismatch

pullRequest.title: Qwen
pullRequest.body:
Qwen Image
contains #998 and #1001

Switch to this PR:
```
git fetch origin pull/1007/head:qwen
git switch qwen
./update.sh or update.bat
```

For those concerned about the size of the model:

- it trains well on any consumer card starting 16 GB of vram and batch size 2
- each step takes a bit longer than Flux/Chroma, but it appears to learn faster (in less steps)
- inference at good quality still at 8 steps with Lightning, maybe even 4 steps: https://huggingface.co/lightx2v/Qwen-Image-Lightning/tree/main
- combined with https://github.com/nunchaku-tech/nunchaku it will be faster than many other models. They already have Lightning for nunchaku, generating images in less than 10 seconds at 1024px on a RTX 4070. Unfortunately, they are still working on LoRA support.
- With Lightning, you can use CFG 1 which makes the model twice as fast.

==> OneTrainer LoRAs work in Comfy and SwarmUI, with Lightning, but not with nunchaku yet.


- [x] masked training broken
- [x] caching with multiple dataloader threads

pullRequest.title: Code Maintenance
pullRequest.body:
This PR contains various code changes *in preparation* of adding the Qwen model, and another PR that provides layer filtering for Full finetuning, which is necessary for Chroma and Qwen.

It is separate to avoid many unrelated changes in those PRs mentioned above.
Included in this PR:

- simplify latent packing for Flux and Chroma (and later Qwen)
- move timestep shifting from the noise mixin and the samplers to the Model, because all models calculate it in the same way (code deduplication), but Qwen uses different parameters than the currently hardcoded ones in the noise mixin
- code deduplication in creating the parameters in each model, in preparation for the layer filter
- code deduplication in setting requires_grad in each model, in preparation for the layer filter
- Chroma code cleanup
- Bugfix for https://github.com/Nerogar/OneTrainer/issues/979, but
- [x] test HiDream sampler

How to switch a PR:
```
git fetch origin pull/998/head:pr-998
git switch pr-998
```

- [x] only show Dynamic Timestep Shifting toggle for those models that support it

Fix or workaround for https://github.com/Nerogar/OneTrainer/issues/1026
depending on https://github.com/huggingface/diffusers/issues/12423

pullRequest.title: Misleading Loss Weight Functions
pullRequest.body:
This draft PR is the bare minimum implementation to address https://github.com/Nerogar/OneTrainer/issues/1121, which I agree with.

However we could further improve it:
 - are there any of the "diffusion" loss weight functions that can be applied to flow matching?
@Koratahiu
 - do any of your recent UI improvements @O-J1 hide dropdown values that cannot be used, or validate values? In that case, this would be another candidate. For now an exception is raised after training start.
 - SIGMA loss weight function isn't used in practice as far as I know. I remember Nerogar saying he only implemented it because it was either in a paper or in another trainer. Does anyone now where this originated from?

pullRequest.title: Check training loss for NaN
pullRequest.body:
Error out if NaN training loss was detected, which is non-recoverable

- it does not react to fp16 overflow. In this case, gradients become `inf` or `NaN', but not the loss (unless the forward pass produces a NaN values, but this case cannot be recovered by the scaler)
- it doesn't prevent NaN gradients to be applied to the model. In case the user has enabled backups before save, that backup will be destroyed. It might be better to avoid that, but: checking for NaN before the each optimizer step requires a CUDA synchronisation because it branches on CPU. this is either a small performance impact, or even a large performance impact if fused backpass is used. So I decided against it.

pullRequest.title: Z-Image
pullRequest.body:
Initial support
```
git fetch origin pull/1195/head:pr-1195
git switch pr-1195
```
then update

Currently the De-Turbo training method is supported: https://huggingface.co/ostris/Z-Image-De-Turbo
This means you have to set up your samples with CFG and at least 20 steps. The resulting LoRA can be used with the Turbo model.

More training methods might be added based on further experiments

Includes https://github.com/Nerogar/OneTrainer/pull/1193 https://github.com/Nerogar/OneTrainer/pull/1176

- [x] needs `attn-only` and `attn-mlp` presets, but naive filters for "attention" and "feed_forward" would also include such layers outside the transformer blocks, in `context_refiner` and `noise_refiner`. This might hurt training - regex preset necessary?
- [x] revert this commit before merge 20c718a611f8cbbb0172fffb12b04144fe1a1072
- [x] enable dynamic timestep shifting
